title: Statement Regarding the Publication of Reference Rates on Friday, April 3, 2026

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The New York Fed announced that TGCR, BGCR, SOFR, and SOFR Averages/Index will not be published on April 3, 2026, due to the March Employment Situation report release coinciding with Good Friday. Repo market participants are expected to treat April 3 as a holiday, with cleared Treasury repo settlement unavailable. The EFFR and OBFR will still be published on April 3 as they follow the NY Fed's separate holiday schedule.
key_points: No publication of TGCR, BGCR, SOFR, or SOFR Averages/Index on April 3, 2026
Cleared Treasury repo settlement is not scheduled for April 3, eliminating two of three market segments used for Treasury repo reference rates
EFFR and OBFR will be published on April 3 per NY Fed's holiday schedule
SIFMA did not recommend full closure of U.S. government securities secondary trading
On April 6, SOFR will reflect transactions settling April 2 and maturing April 6
why_it_matters: SOFR is the primary benchmark for trillions of dollars in loans and derivatives. Its temporary non-publication during a major data release demonstrates how critical reference rate infrastructure adapts to market events. The divergence between EFFR/OBFR (published) and repo-based rates (not published) on the same day creates a temporary gap in rate coverage that market participants must account for.
macro_context: The April 3 reference rate change is directly tied to the March Employment Situation report release, a major monthly macro catalyst. SIFMA's partial market treatment reflects the dual nature of the date as both a federal holiday and a scheduled data release.
market_structure_context: The announcement clarifies how reference rate infrastructure handles holiday scheduling when major economic data releases occur. The loss of cleared repo settlement eliminates two of three Treasury repo market segments from rate calculations, affecting SOFR composition.
notes: Source is high-authority institutional announcement from NY Fed. URL not provided in metadata. The body contains substantive market structure details about reference rate publication procedures during the March Employment Situation release. All key facts are directly supported by source text.
